consumer_complaint_narrative: Synchrony charged me a $50 monthly maintenance fee on my Ally Savings that was supposed to be free. When I called to dispute, they said I did not maintain the minimum balance of $1000, even though my statements show I was always above that threshold. I want all fees refunded and a guarantee this will not happen again.

Someone made 6 fraudulent purchases on my Chase Sapphire card totaling $20000. I reported this to American Express immediately and they opened an investigation. After 2 weeks, they denied my fraud claim saying the transactions appeared legitimate. I have filed a police report and have evidence that I was in a different state when these transactions occurred.

Ally Bank charged me a $100 monthly maintenance fee on my Chase Savings that was supposed to be free. When I called to dispute, they said I did not maintain the minimum balance of $1500, even though my statements show I was always above that threshold. I want all fees refunded and a guarantee this will not happen again.

The rewards program on my Capital One Venture card was changed without notice. Capital One devalued the points by nearly 24%, making the annual fee of $250 no longer worth it. I specifically chose this card for its rewards structure and feel this is a bait-and-switch tactic. Long-time customers should be grandfathered into the original rewards program.

Synchrony closed my Ally Savings account without warning, citing 'business reasons.' I had $1500 in the account and it took 2 weeks to receive a cashier's check. No explanation was ever provided for the closure. I have been a customer for 8 years with no suspicious activity on the account.

My Discover Savings with Chase was accessed by someone who changed my contact information and attempted to transfer $100 out of my account. When I reported this, Chase took 5 weeks to investigate and temporarily restricted my access. I am concerned about the security of their online banking platform and how this person gained access.

I opened a Ally Savings with Ally Bank because they advertised a 24.6% APY. After three months, the rate was quietly dropped to 6.1% without notification. When I called, they said the promotional rate had expired, but this was never clearly communicated at account opening. I moved $1500 into this account specifically for the higher rate.

I sent $250 via PayPal through PayPal to pay my landlord. The money was deducted from my account but never received by the recipient. It has been 8 weeks and PayPal has not been able to locate the funds or provide a refund. I am now at risk of eviction because my rent appears unpaid.

I accidentally sent $500 to the wrong person through Venmo via Western Union. I realized the error within 58 minutes and immediately contacted them to cancel. They said the transaction was already completed and they cannot reverse it. They suggested I 'ask the recipient nicely' to send it back. This is an unacceptable response for such a large amount.

My Marcus Savings with Ally Bank was accessed by someone who changed my contact information and attempted to transfer $500 out of my account. When I reported this, Ally Bank took 8 weeks to investigate and temporarily restricted my access. I am concerned about the security of their online banking platform and how this person gained access.

I tried to withdraw $50 from my Synchrony Savings at Ally Bank and was told my account was frozen pending a security review. No one informed me of any issues beforehand. I had bills due and no access to my own money for 3 weeks. The customer service was unhelpful and could not tell me when the review would be completed.

Discover charged me a $75 late fee on my Amex Gold card even though I made my payment on time. My bank records show the payment was initiated 7 days before the due date. When I called to dispute, they said the payment was received one day late due to processing time. This is unacceptable — I should not be penalized for their slow processing system.
